section_title: When is the enforcement date of section 241 of the CA 2016?
content:
Q: When is the enforcement date of section 241 of the CA 2016?

A: Section 241 of the CA 2016 comes into force on 15 March 2019.

section_title: What is the principle of section 241?
content:
Q: What is the principle of section 241?

A: Section 241 of the CA 2016 requires any person who is qualified to act as a secretary
and who desires to act as a secretary to register with the Registrar before he can act
as a secretary.
The Registrar will issue a practising certificate under section 241 upon satisfaction on
the qualification and requirements specified under the CA 2016, the Companies
(Practising Certificate for Secretaries) Regulations 2019 and the Guidelines Relating to
Practising Certificate for Secretaries.

section_title: Who is required to register pursuant to section 241 of the CA2016?
content:
Q: Who is required to register pursuant to section 241 of the CA2016?

A: All qualified person under section 235 of CA 2016 who wish to act as a secretary
irrespective of their professional background.

section_title: Can a person who has been appointed as a secretary of a company prior to the enforcement of section 241 of the CA 2016 c
content:
Q: Can a person who has been appointed as a secretary of a company prior to the enforcement of section 241 of the CA 2016 continue to act as a secretary?

A: Yes, he may continue to act as a secretary to the company but is still required to register
with the Registrar within 12 months from the enforcement date.

section_title: What will happen if a person who is not a citizen or permanent resident of Malaysia but has been appointed as a secretar
content:
Q: What will happen if a person who is not a citizen or permanent resident of Malaysia but has been appointed as a secretary prior to 31 January 2017?

A: He may apply for practising certificate subject to the following conditions:
(a) he shall only act as a secretary to the companies of which he was appointed
before 31 January 2017;
(b) he shall not accept any new appointment as a secretary; and
he has complied with the requirements under paragraph 4(a), (b) and (d) in the
Guidelines Relating To Practising Certificate For Secretaries Under Section 241 Of
The Companies Act 2016.

section_title: How long is the validity period of the practicing certificate?
content:
Q: How long is the validity period of the practicing certificate?

A: The first practising certificate shall be valid for a period of one year from the date of
issuance.
The renewal of the practising certificate shall be valid for a period of not more than
three years.
Part K - Page 3

section_title: What is the time frame for existing company secretary to apply for a practising certificate?
content:
Q: What is the time frame for existing company secretary to apply for a practising certificate?

A: An existing company secretary who is being named as secretary for companies has 12
months period i.e. until 14 March 2020 to apply for practising certificate.
To avoid any delay SSM encourages secretary to make an early application.

section_title: When executing any document in his capacity as a secretary or when lodging any document or in correspondences with the R
content:
Q: When executing any document in his capacity as a secretary or when lodging any document or in correspondences with the Registrar, which number should be used to describe the secretary's ID? Is it his LS/ prescribed bodies (PB) membership number OR his practising certificate number?

A: During the transition period of twelve months, a secretary may state his LS
number/prescribed bodies (PB) membership number OR the practising certificate
number when executing any document in his capacity as a secretary or when lodging
Part K - Page 4
any document or in correspondences with the Registrar.
After the transition period, a secretary must state the practising certificate number
when executing any document in his capacity as a secretary or when lodging any
document or in correspondences with the Registrar.

section_title: Is it mandatory for a qualified person who act as a secretary to register with SSM under section 241 of the Companies Ac
content:
Q: Is it mandatory for a qualified person who act as a secretary to register with SSM under section 241 of the Companies Act 2016 before he can act as a secretary after the Act come into operation on 31 January 2017?

A: No. For the time being, the qualified person does not require to register as a company
secretary with SSM since Companies Act 2016 comes into operation on 31 January
2016 except for section 241 and Division 8 of Part III of the Act.

section_title: Is a copy of the email notification accepted as a supporting document? (updated on 13 March 2023)
content:
Q: Is a copy of the email notification accepted as a supporting document? (updated on 13 March 2023)

A: A copy of the email can be used as an additional supporting document if the company
secretary can prove that the email was their usual medium of communication.

section_title: If there is proof of receipt of a copy of the notice of resignation served by the company secretary to the director via
content:
Q: If there is proof of receipt of a copy of the notice of resignation served by the company secretary to the director via registered letter, can the company secretary still resign through section 237(2)?

A: The company secretary cannot resign through section 237(2) because the directors are
still contactable

section_title: If the company secretary resigns through lodgement of section 237(3)(a), is the company secretary's name still in the SS
content:
Q: If the company secretary resigns through lodgement of section 237(3)(a), is the company secretary's name still in the SSM records? (updated on 13 March 2023)

A: Yes, the company secretary's name is still displayed on the company's corporate profile
until his resignation is updated by the company under section 58.
